segments.text: IKEA saved millions of dollars by
deleting air. It sounds crazy, but the
proof is actually hiding [music] inside
the bag of tea lightss. See, IKEA used
to sell these candles loose in a bag.
[music] To us, that looks normal. But
for a shipping company, that's a
disaster. That jumble creates [music]
random gaps of empty spaces, and moving
empty space was costing a fortune in
fuels and trucks. So, they engineered
this, the brick, by stacking the candles
and vacuum sealing them. They removed
the air completely. It seems tiny, but
the logistics data was shocking. The
specific shape fits 30% more [music]
candles on every single pallet. The
efficiency allowed them to eliminate 400
truck trips every single year. That's
massive. They stopped shipping here and
started shipping profits. For more
innovative content, subscribe.
